---
title: "Cross-model agreement"
subtitle: "Where the three LLMs converge and where they diverge"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source("_data.R")
library(ggplot2); library(scales); library(tidyr)
models <- available_models()
m_aggs <- map(names(models), load_model_agg) |> setNames(names(models))
# Stack models, doc_id × dim × model in long form
long <- bind_rows(map(names(m_aggs), function(mid) {
  a <- m_aggs[[mid]]; if (is.null(a)) return(NULL)
  a |> pivot_longer(cols = any_of(V4_DIMS), names_to = "dim", values_to = "score") |>
    mutate(model = mid)
}))
# Wide per (doc × dim) — one col per model
wide <- long |>
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(doc_id, dim), names_from = model, values_from = score)
```

## Per-dimension pairwise correlations

```{r cor-grid, echo=FALSE}
library(knitr)
model_pairs <- combn(names(models), 2, simplify = FALSE)
cor_tbl <- map_dfr(V4_DIMS, function(d) {
  sub <- wide |> filter(dim == d)
  map_dfr(model_pairs, function(p) {
    if (!all(p %in% names(sub))) return(tibble())
    x <- sub[[p[1]]]; y <- sub[[p[2]]]
    keep <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
    if (sum(keep) < 30) return(tibble())
    tibble(
      dim = d,
      label = DIM_LABELS[d],
      pair = paste(p, collapse = " ↔ "),
      n = sum(keep),
      pearson = round(cor(x[keep], y[keep], method = "pearson"), 3),
      spearman = round(cor(x[keep], y[keep], method = "spearman"), 3),
      mean_abs_gap = round(mean(abs(x[keep] - y[keep])), 2)
    )
  })
})
cor_tbl |>
  select(Dimension = label, Pair = pair, n, Pearson = pearson, Spearman = spearman, `Mean |gap|` = mean_abs_gap) |>
  kable()
```

## Disagreement-flagged outliers

Manifestos where the three models disagree by more than 3 points on
`populism_overall`:

```{r outliers, echo=FALSE}
manifestos <- load_manifestos()
mat <- wide |> filter(dim == "populism_overall")
score_cols <- intersect(names(models), names(mat))
mat$rng <- apply(mat[score_cols], 1, function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]; if (length(x) < 2) NA else diff(range(x))
})
out <- mat |> filter(rng > 3) |>
  left_join(manifestos |> select(doc_id, countryname, partyabbrev, year, language), by = "doc_id") |>
  arrange(desc(rng))

if (nrow(out) == 0) {
  cat("*No high-divergence cases detected — the three models broadly agree.*\n")
} else {
  out |> mutate(across(all_of(score_cols), ~ round(.x, 1))) |>
    select(doc_id, Country = countryname, Party = partyabbrev, Year = year, Lang = language,
           any_of(score_cols), Range = rng) |>
    head(40) |> kable()
}
```

## Score distribution by model

```{r dist, echo=FALSE, fig.height=8}
library(ggplot2)
plot_dims <- c("populism_overall","pop_anti_elitism","pop_people_centrism","pop_manichean",
               "liberalism_overall","lib_political","lib_economic","lib_financial_market")
long |> filter(dim %in% plot_dims, is.finite(score)) |>
  mutate(dim_lbl = factor(DIM_LABELS[dim], levels = DIM_LABELS[plot_dims]),
         model_lbl = factor(model, levels = names(models),
                            labels = map_chr(models, "short"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = model_lbl)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "identity", alpha = 0.5, colour = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~dim_lbl, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Model") +
  labs(x = "Score (0–10)", y = "Manifestos") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```
